site.name: Shovon Mandal
site.title: Lecturer, Department of Computer Science and Engineering
site.affiliation: Northern University of Business and Technology Khulna, Bangladesh
site.aspiration: Seeking M.Sc./Ph.D. research supervision and RA/TA-supported graduate opportunities in secure distributed systems, blockchain-enabled payment security, NFC/IoT transaction security, and implementation-oriented computing. Prepared to contribute through prototype development, technical writing, teaching support, data organization, and automation-driven research workflows.
site.headline: Prospective M.Sc./Ph.D. Researcher · Secure Digital Payments · Blockchain/NFC Transaction Security · Academic Automation

about: I am a Computer Science and Engineering lecturer and graduate researcher with a research trajectory in blockchain-based secure digital payments, NFC-enabled transaction security, secure distributed systems, and implementation-oriented computing.

My academic work connects research publication, thesis-based prototype development, and classroom teaching. I have worked on secure cashless payment systems, blockchain/NFC interaction, IOTA-based transaction demonstration, and decentralized communication concepts.

I am also interested in academic workflow automation. In my teaching environment, I have developed practical systems using Google Sheets, Apps Script, and Python to simplify class routine management, student communication, assessment record organization, and administrative coordination.

For a prospective supervisor or research group, my profile offers a combination of teaching experience, implementation ability, publication experience, technical documentation, and readiness to support research, lab, and teaching activities.

highlightCards.title: Academic Systems Mindset

projects.title: Blockchain and NFC-Based Secure Cashless Payment Prototype
projects.description: A secure cashless payment prototype integrating blockchain-based transaction verification with NFC-enabled payment interaction.
highlights: Implemented Arduino and Python modules for NFC-based transaction processing.
Supported experimental validation of secure cashless payment workflows.
Connected implementation evidence with blockchain and NFC transaction security research.

projects.title: IOTA-Based Payment Website and Decentralized Transaction Demonstration
projects.description: A web-based prototype demonstrating IOTA-supported payment interaction and decentralized transaction flow.
highlights: Developed front-end and backend components for payment demonstration and user interaction.
Linked implementation work to decentralized transaction and node-confirmation research.
Demonstrated applied interest in distributed ledger systems.

projects.title: Academic Routine Management System using Google Sheets and Apps Script
projects.description: A department-level class routine management system built with Google Sheets and Apps Script to support course-offering based routine preparation, faculty/section-wise routine retrieval, protected routine views, and PDF routine downloads.
highlights: Automated section, teacher, and course-code selection from course offerings.
Supported faculty-wise and section-wise routine extraction for academic use.
Included sheet protection, installable trigger support, individual PDF export, and merged PDF workflow.

projects.title: Python-Based Discord Server Management Bot for Academic Communication
projects.description: A Python-based Discord bot designed for academic server management, student verification, role assignment, nickname formatting, access control, reminders, attendance logging, and admin communication.
highlights: Automated student verification and section-based role assignment.
Improved academic communication and student access management.
Used environment-variable based configuration and security-focused repository structure.

additionalProjects.title: Student Management Android Application
additionalProjects.description: Android Studio-based application designed for student information management and routine academic record workflows.

additionalProjects.title: Google Sheets-Based Marking and Academic Management Workflows
additionalProjects.description: Spreadsheet-driven systems used to keep marking, assessment records, and academic management cleaner, more structured, and easier to maintain.

additionalProjects.title: Traffic Light System
additionalProjects.description: Hardware-oriented project demonstrating control logic, digital system behavior, and implementation concepts.

additionalProjects.title: Fire Alarm Integrated with Mobile SIM
additionalProjects.description: A hardware project combining alert functionality with mobile SIM-based communication and notification concepts.

graduateFit.title: Research Alignment
graduateFit.label: Supervisor Fit
graduateFit.text: My strongest fit is with research groups working on secure distributed systems, blockchain security, FinTech security, NFC/IoT transaction systems, decentralized applications, or applied computing systems.

graduateFit.title: Evidence of Preparation
graduateFit.label: Academic Evidence
graduateFit.text: My profile includes thesis work on blockchain-based payment systems, accepted/published conference papers, secure payment prototypes, and a strong undergraduate academic record with Highest Distinction.

graduateFit.title: RA/TA Readiness
graduateFit.label: Assistantship Fit
graduateFit.text: As a CSE lecturer, I can contribute to teaching assistance, lab support, student mentoring, grading workflows, technical documentation, and organized course/research support.

graduateFit.title: Implementation Strength
graduateFit.label: Technical Fit
graduateFit.text: I can build working systems using Python, Java, Google Apps Script, web technologies, Arduino/NFC modules, and automation tools, which helps convert research ideas into usable prototypes.
